examples.sentence: この曲を聞くと、歌わずにはいられない。
examples.translation: When I hear this song, I can’t help but sing.
examples.translationEn: When I hear this song, I can’t help but sing.
examples.romaji: Kono kyoku o kiku to, utawazu ni wa irarenai.

examples.sentence: 彼女が泣いているのを見ると、慰めずにはいられない。
examples.translation: When I see her crying, I can’t resist comforting her.
examples.translationEn: When I see her crying, I can’t resist comforting her.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga naite iru no o miru to, nagusamezu ni wa irarenai.

examples.sentence: この小説は面白いので、読まずにはいられない。
examples.translation: This novel is so interesting that I can’t help but read it.
examples.translationEn: This novel is so interesting that I can’t help but read it.
examples.romaji: Kono shousetsu wa omoshiroi no de, yomazu ni wa irarenai.

examples.sentence: 彼はおいしい料理を前にすると、食べずにはいられない。
examples.translation: When he’s faced with delicious food, he can’t help but eat it.
examples.translationEn: When he’s faced with delicious food, he can’t help but eat it.
examples.romaji: Kare wa oishii ryouri o mae ni suru to, tabezu ni wa irarenai.

examples.sentence: この雨は止みそうにない。
examples.translation: It doesn't seem like this rain will stop.
examples.translationEn: It doesn't seem like this rain will stop.
examples.romaji: Kono ame wa yami sou ni nai.

examples.sentence: 彼が試験に合格しそうにない。
examples.translation: It doesn't look like he'll pass the exam.
examples.translationEn: It doesn't look like he'll pass the exam.
examples.romaji: Kare ga shiken ni goukaku shi sou ni nai.

examples.sentence: この料理がおいしそうにない。
examples.translation: This dish doesn't look like it will be tasty.
examples.translationEn: This dish doesn't look like it will be tasty.
examples.romaji: Kono ryouri ga oishisou ni nai.

examples.sentence: この部屋はきれいそうにない。
examples.translation: This room doesn't seem likely to be clean.
examples.translationEn: This room doesn't seem likely to be clean.
examples.romaji: Kono heya wa kirei sou ni nai.

examples.sentence: 電話をかけたかと思ったら、すぐに彼女が出た。
examples.translation: No sooner had I called than she picked up.
examples.translationEn: No sooner had I called than she picked up.
examples.romaji: Denwa o kaketa ka to omottara, sugu ni kanojo ga deta.

examples.sentence: 離陸したかと思ったら、飛行機が揺れ始めた。
examples.translation: As soon as we took off, the plane started shaking.
examples.translationEn: As soon as we took off, the plane started shaking.
examples.romaji: Ririku shita ka to omottara, hikouki ga yure hajimeta.

examples.sentence: 買い物に行ったかと思ったら、雨が降ってきた。
examples.translation: No sooner had I gone shopping than it began to rain.
examples.translationEn: No sooner had I gone shopping than it began to rain.
examples.romaji: Kaimono ni itta ka to omottara, ame ga futte kita.

examples.sentence: 仕事を終わったかと思ったら、上司が追加の仕事を持ってきた。
examples.translation: As soon as I finished work, my boss brought more tasks.
examples.translationEn: As soon as I finished work, my boss brought more tasks.
examples.romaji: Shigoto o owatta ka to omottara, joushi ga tsuika no shigoto o motte kita.

examples.sentence: 彼は会社を辞めたきり、連絡がない。
examples.translation: Ever since he quit the company, there's been no contact.
examples.translationEn: Ever since he quit the company, there's been no contact.
examples.romaji: Kare wa kaisha o yameta kiri, renraku ga nai.

examples.sentence: 昨日の朝、メールを送ったきり、返事がない。
examples.translation: I sent an email yesterday morning, and there's been no reply since then.
examples.translationEn: I sent an email yesterday morning, and there's been no reply since then.
examples.romaji: Kinō no asa, mēru o okutta kiri, henji ga nai.

examples.sentence: 子供のころ海に行ったきりで、もう20年になる。
examples.translation: It's been 20 years since I last went to the sea as a child.
examples.translationEn: It's been 20 years since I last went to the sea as a child.
examples.romaji: Kodomo no koro umi ni itta kiri de, mō nijūnen ni naru.

examples.sentence: 彼女と別れたきり、彼女のことを考えなくなった。
examples.translation: Ever since I broke up with her, I stopped thinking about her.
examples.translationEn: Ever since I broke up with her, I stopped thinking about her.
examples.romaji: Kanojo to wakareta kiri, kanojo no koto o kangaenaku natta.

examples.sentence: 彼は日本語が上手だけあって、日本人のように話すことができる。
examples.translation: As expected from his good Japanese skills, he can speak like a native.
examples.translationEn: As expected from his good Japanese skills, he can speak like a native.
examples.romaji: Kare wa nihongo ga jōzu dake atte, nihonjin no yō ni hanasu koto ga dekiru.

examples.sentence: このレストランは有名だけあって、料理がとても美味しい。
examples.translation: No wonder this restaurant is famous; the food is very delicious.
examples.translationEn: No wonder this restaurant is famous; the food is very delicious.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa yūmei dake atte, ryōri ga totemo oishii.

examples.sentence: 彼女は経験豊富なだけあって、その問題を簡単に解決できた。
examples.translation: It's natural that she's experienced; she solved the problem easily.
examples.translationEn: It's natural that she's experienced; she solved the problem easily.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa keiken hōfu na dake atte, sono mondai o kantan ni kaiketsu dekita.

examples.sentence: 彼は優秀な学生だけあって、いつもテストの点数が高い。
examples.translation: As expected of an excellent student, he always scores high on tests.
examples.translationEn: As expected of an excellent student, he always scores high on tests.
examples.romaji: Kare wa yūshū na gakusei dake atte, itsumo tesuto no tensū ga takai.

examples.sentence: 失敗するだけましだ。もっと大変なことにならなくてよかった。
examples.translation: At least we only failed—it could’ve been much worse.
examples.translationEn: At least we only failed—it could’ve been much worse.
examples.romaji: Shippai suru dake mashi da. Motto taihen na koto ni naranakute yokatta.

examples.sentence: 遅れるだけましだ。来られないよりはいい。
examples.translation: At least I’m only late. It’s better than not being able to come at all.
examples.translationEn: At least I’m only late. It’s better than not being able to come at all.
examples.romaji: Okureru dake mashi da. Korarenai yori wa ii.

examples.sentence: 小さな部屋だけましだ。全く部屋がないよりはいい。
examples.translation: At least we have a small room; it’s better than having no room at all.
examples.translationEn: At least we have a small room; it’s better than having no room at all.
examples.romaji: Chīsana heya dake mashi da. Mattaku heya ga nai yori wa ii.

examples.sentence: 彼が結婚しないだけましだ。大変なことになるところだったよ。
examples.translation: At least he’s not getting married—things could have gotten complicated.
examples.translationEn: At least he’s not getting married—things could have gotten complicated.
examples.romaji: Kare ga kekkon shinai dake mashi da. Taihen na koto ni naru tokoro datta yo.

examples.sentence: 掃除したところ、部屋がずっとキレイになりました。
examples.translation: I just finished cleaning, and the room is so much cleaner now.
examples.translationEn: I just finished cleaning, and the room is so much cleaner now.
examples.romaji: Sōji shita tokoro, heya ga zutto kirei ni narimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼に聞いたところ、その質問の答えは分からないみたいです。
examples.translation: I just asked him, and it seems he doesn't know the answer.
examples.translationEn: I just asked him, and it seems he doesn't know the answer.
examples.romaji: Kare ni kiita tokoro, sono shitsumon no kotae wa wakaranai mitai desu.

examples.sentence: 宿題を出したところ、先生が間違いを見つけました。
examples.translation: As soon as I turned in my homework, the teacher found a mistake.
examples.translationEn: As soon as I turned in my homework, the teacher found a mistake.
examples.romaji: Shukudai o dashita tokoro, sensei ga machigai o mitsukemashita.

examples.sentence: 昼ごはんを食べたところ、気分が良くなりました。
examples.translation: Right after eating lunch, I started feeling better.
examples.translationEn: Right after eating lunch, I started feeling better.
examples.romaji: Hirugohan o tabeta tokoro, kibun ga yoku narimashita.

examples.sentence: ドアを開けたとたん、雨が降り始めた。
examples.translation: As soon as I opened the door, it started raining.
examples.translationEn: As soon as I opened the door, it started raining.
examples.romaji: Doa o aketa totan, ame ga furi-hajimeta.

examples.sentence: 電話をかけたとたん、バッテリーが切れた。
examples.translation: Just when I made the phone call, the battery died.
examples.translationEn: Just when I made the phone call, the battery died.
examples.romaji: Denwa o kaketa totan, batterī ga kireta.

examples.sentence: 彼女に会ったとたん、思い出の歌が流れた。
examples.translation: No sooner had I met her than a nostalgic song started playing.
examples.translationEn: No sooner had I met her than a nostalgic song started playing.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ni atta totan, omoide no uta ga nagareta.

examples.sentence: テストが終わったとたん、リラックスできた。
examples.translation: The moment the test ended, I was able to relax.
examples.translationEn: The moment the test ended, I was able to relax.
examples.romaji: Tesuto ga owatta totan, rirakkusu dekita.
